task_yaml.task:
I'm building a tiny price/metadata tracker for a few PC games and I'd rather
pull the data from an API than scrape Steam store pages by hand.

Start from this Steam store page:

    https://store.steampowered.com/app/1245620/ELDEN_RING/

Steam's storefront is backed by a JSON endpoint that the store page itself
calls to render game details — it isn't really documented, so you'll need to
find it (the app id in the URL is the key input). Please discover that
endpoint and use it (not by parsing the store page HTML) to retrieve, for
this game:

- the official title,
- the developer(s) and publisher(s),
- which platforms are supported (Windows / macOS / Linux),
- whether it's free, and the current price if it isn't,
- the release date,
- the Metacritic score if present.

Save the structured result to `/tmp_workspace/results/steam_appdetails.json`,
and save the exact request URL you called plus its raw JSON response to
`/tmp_workspace/results/steam_evidence/` so the pull is reproducible. A short
note on how you found the endpoint is welcome.
